§6Flight Mechanics§f

§aTake complete control over your aerial movements and master the flight hotkeys!§f
Flying a jetpack requires more than just holding the jump key. To prevent high-speed crashes and manage your energy reserves efficiently, you must customize and learn your cockpit controls.

§6Cockpit Controls§f:
Check your game's keybind menu to customize the following active hotkeys:
§b• Engine Toggle§f: Turns the jetpack's rocket engine on or off. Turn it off when walking to prevent accidental fuel drain.
§b• Hover Mode§f: Toggles hover stabilizer. When active, you will float suspended in mid-air, drifting slowly down or holding your height perfectly depending on the jetpack tier. Hovering is highly efficient and perfect for building, mining high-altitude ores, or precise combat.
§b• Emergency Hover§f: If your engine is on but you run out of fuel mid-air, the jetpack will enter an emergency descent mode to prevent lethal fall damage.

§6Charging & Safety§f:
Jetpacks do not recharge automatically. Place your jetpack inside a compatible RF energy charger (such as a NuclearCraft charging block or a thermal power buffer) to fill its internal capacitors.

§cWarning§f: Flying consumes heavy RF currents. Keep a close eye on your HUD energy bar or armor tooltip to ensure you do not lose thrust while flying over deep lava lakes or radioactive zones.

§eTip§f: Coils, capacitors, and thrusters are essential crafting components for every jetpack tier. Craft a Basic Coil to begin.
